output.summary: Three-part change plan: (1) Fix accessibility gaps — missing aria-label on tablist, missing aria-controls on tab buttons in server-rendered PHP, (2) Rebuild test page using DemoContainer wrappers with comprehensive coverage including edge cases, (3) Add per-tab color attribute to TabsItem that applies a color class wrapper affecting both the tablist button and panel background, falling back to page accent color when unset.

requirements: Tablist container must have role='tablist' (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Tablist must have aria-label or aria-labelledby (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Tablist must have aria-orientation='vertical' for vertical orientation (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Tab elements must have role='tab' (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Tab elements must have aria-controls referencing tabpanel id (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Tab elements must have aria-selected='true' for active, 'false' for inactive (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Tabpanel elements must have role='tabpanel' (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Tabpanel elements must have aria-labelledby referencing tab id (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Keyboard: ArrowDown/ArrowUp for vertical tablist navigation (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Keyboard: Home/End to jump to first/last tab (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Keyboard: Space/Enter to activate tab (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Keyboard: Tab key enters/exits tablist (WAI-ARIA Tabs)
Mobile disclosure: native <details>/<summary> provides Enter/Space keyboard interaction (WAI-ARIA Disclosure)
Mobile disclosure: summary element acts as button with implicit aria-expanded (WAI-ARIA Disclosure)
Tabpanel roles must be removed in mobile mode to avoid conflicting with native details/summary semantics (architectural requirement)
Desktop mode must prevent summary click toggling (architectural requirement)
currentStatus: PASS: role='tablist' is set on the tablist container in PHP
PASS: aria-orientation='vertical' is set on the tablist in PHP
PASS: role='tab' is set on tab buttons in PHP
PASS: aria-selected is correctly set (true for first, false for rest) in PHP
PASS: tabindex roving (0 for active, -1 for inactive) is set in PHP
PASS: Tab button IDs are generated in PHP (tab-{baseId}-{index})
PASS: JS setupAria() sets aria-controls on tab buttons linking to panel IDs
PASS: JS setupAria() sets role='tabpanel', tabindex='0', aria-labelledby on content panels
PASS: JS keyboard navigation implements ArrowDown/ArrowUp with wrapping
PASS: JS keyboard navigation implements Home/End
PASS: JS keyboard navigation implements Space/Enter activation
PASS: JS setMobileMode() removes tabpanel roles, aria-labelledby, and tabindex from panels
PASS: JS setDesktopMode() restores tabpanel roles
PASS: JS prevents summary click on desktop
PASS: Mobile uses native <details>/<summary> for disclosure behavior
PASS: Desktop ensures at least one tab is always open

gaps.requirement: Tablist must have aria-label or aria-labelledby
gaps.currentState: The tablist div has role='tablist' and aria-orientation='vertical' but no aria-label or aria-labelledby. The WAI-ARIA Tabs pattern requires the tablist to be labeled.
gaps.fix: Add aria-label to the tablist. Use the block heading as the label value. In PHP: aria-label="{heading}". If no heading is set, use a generic label like 'Tabs'.

gaps.requirement: Tab buttons must have aria-controls referencing tabpanel id in server-rendered HTML
gaps.currentState: aria-controls is only set by JS in setupAria(). Before JS runs (or if JS fails), tab buttons have no aria-controls. The PHP template generates tab button IDs but does not set aria-controls or generate panel IDs.
gaps.fix: Add aria-controls to tab buttons in PHP, and add id/role/aria-labelledby to content panels in TabsItem.php. This provides proper ARIA in server-rendered HTML before JS enhances it. The JS setupAria() can still run but will be setting values that already exist (idempotent).

fileChanges.description: Add tabColor attribute for per-tab color selection
fileChanges.details: Add new attribute: "tabColor": { "type": "string", "default": "" }. Empty string means 'inherit from page accent'. Valid values: '', 'neon-green', 'blue', 'green', 'yellow', 'orange', 'purple'. Also add "usesContext": ["takt/tabs/baseId"] so TabsItem can generate matching panel IDs in PHP.

fileChanges.description: Add tabsColor sync array and providesContext for baseId
fileChanges.details: Add: "tabsColor": { "type": "array", "default": [] } to sync child tabColor values to parent (same pattern as tabsHeading/tabsDescription). Add "providesContext": { "takt/tabs/baseId": "anchor" } so children can generate matching panel IDs.

fileChanges.description: Fix accessibility gaps and add per-tab color support
fileChanges.details: 1. Add aria-label to tablist div using heading value (fallback to 'Tabs'). 2. Add aria-controls on each tab button pointing to panel ID (aria-controls="{baseId}-panel-{index}"). 3. For per-tab color: wrap each tab button in a span with the color class if tabsColor[index] is set, OR apply the color class directly to the button element. The color class (e.g., 'blue') will override --accent-color CSS custom properties, making bg-accent and bg-accent-lighter use the tab's color. 4. Pass baseId and tabColor to child blocks via context so TabsItem.php can generate matching IDs and apply color to the panel.

fileChanges.description: Sync tabsColor array and add color picker in editor
fileChanges.details: 1. Add tabsColor to the sync effect (same pattern as tabsHeading/tabsDescription). 2. Apply color class to tab buttons in the editor preview. 3. Add aria-label to tablist div in editor. The color picker UI will be in the TabsItem sidebar (InspectorControls), not in Tabs.tsx.

fileChanges.description: Add color picker control in InspectorControls sidebar
fileChanges.details: Add InspectorControls with a color selection dropdown/radio group for tabColor attribute. Options: 'Default (Page Accent)', 'Neon Green', 'Blue', 'Green', 'Yellow', 'Orange', 'Purple'. Apply the selected color class to the <details> wrapper in the editor.

fileChanges.description: Fix accessibility gaps and add per-tab color support
fileChanges.details: 1. Add id, role='tabpanel', aria-labelledby, and tabindex='0' to the .tabs-item-content div in the server-rendered HTML (so ARIA is present before JS). These use the baseId context from the parent. 2. Apply the tabColor class to the <details> element wrapper so the color overrides --accent-color for that tab's panel background (bg-accent-lighter becomes the tab's color-lighter).

fileChanges.description: Update JS to handle per-tab color classes on tab switch
fileChanges.details: 1. When selecting a tab, if the tab button has a color class, apply that color class to the panels container (or to the active panel). Remove color classes from panels container when switching tabs, then apply the new active tab's color class. 2. Read color data from a data attribute on each tab button (e.g., data-tab-color='blue'). 3. The setupAria function already sets IDs; ensure it doesn't conflict with PHP-rendered IDs (use the same ID generation pattern so JS is idempotent).

fileChanges.description: Add CSS for per-tab color transitions if needed
fileChanges.details: May need a small CSS file for smooth color transitions on the panels container when switching tabs. Register in block.json via style field. Only create if transition effects are needed beyond what Tailwind provides.

attributeChanges.name: tabColor
attributeChanges.description: Color override for this tab. Empty string inherits page accent. Valid values: '', 'neon-green', 'blue', 'green', 'yellow', 'orange', 'purple'.

attributeChanges.name: tabsColor
attributeChanges.description: Array of tab color values synced from child tabColor attributes. Same sync pattern as tabsHeading/tabsDescription.

designDecisions.question: How should per-tab color be applied to the panels container?
designDecisions.recommendation: Apply the color class to the panels container div (.tabs-panels) rather than to individual <details> elements. This way the bg-accent-lighter on the panels container changes to the active tab's color. On tab switch, JS removes all color classes from the panels container and applies the new active tab's color class. This keeps the implementation simple and consistent with the existing accent color system.
designDecisions.decision: Apply color class to .tabs-panels container, toggled by JS on tab switch
designDecisions.evidence: The existing color system uses CSS class wrappers (e.g., .blue, .orange) that set --accent-color custom properties. Applying the class to the panels container means bg-accent-lighter and any accent utilities inside the panel will automatically use the correct color without additional CSS.

designDecisions.question: How should per-tab color be applied to tablist buttons?
designDecisions.recommendation: Each tab button should have a data-tab-color attribute with the color value. The button's bg-accent class (applied on active state) should be wrapped in the color class scope. In PHP, wrap the button in a span/div with the color class, or apply the color class directly to the button. The latter is cleaner. JS should toggle the color class on the button alongside the bg-accent class.
designDecisions.decision: Apply color class directly to the tab button element. JS manages toggling it alongside existing state classes.
designDecisions.evidence: Since bg-accent uses var(--accent-color), applying the color class (e.g., 'blue') to the button element will override --accent-color in that scope, making the button background use the correct per-tab color.

designDecisions.question: Should mobile accordion items also reflect per-tab color?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes. The <details> element already uses open:bg-accent and open:border-accent. Adding the color class to the <details> wrapper will make these utilities use the per-tab color. This happens naturally if tabColor is applied as a class on the <details> element in TabsItem.php.
designDecisions.decision: Apply tabColor class to the <details> element in TabsItem.php so mobile accordion items reflect per-tab color
designDecisions.evidence: open:bg-accent uses var(--accent-color). The color class wrapper (e.g., 'blue') overrides --accent-color. So <details class='tabs-item blue ...'> will have the correct color on mobile open state.

designDecisions.question: How should the color picker UI look in the editor?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use a simple select/dropdown in InspectorControls (PanelBody > SelectControl) with options: Default (inherits page accent), Neon Green, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange, Purple. This is consistent with how the theme handles color selection elsewhere.
designDecisions.decision: SelectControl in InspectorControls sidebar for TabsItem
designDecisions.evidence: The theme uses color classes as a flat enum. A SelectControl is the simplest and most discoverable UI pattern.

designDecisions.question: Should the tablist aria-label use the heading text or a generic label?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use the heading text when available (aria-label="{heading}"), falling back to a generic 'Tabs' if no heading is set. This gives screen reader users contextual information about what the tabs contain.
designDecisions.decision: Use heading text for aria-label on tablist, fallback to 'Tabs'
designDecisions.evidence: WAI-ARIA Tabs pattern requires tablist to have aria-label or aria-labelledby. Using the block's heading provides meaningful context.
